title: Handle completed tasks gracefully in resume command
Add 'watch' or 'resume' mode to CLI for existing tasks
Fix Task ID persistence on exit and rebuild client
Research and implement Go library for Gemini Interactions API
description: Create a Go client library for the Gemini Interactions REST API based on the documentation in docs/interactions_api.md. Use GEMINI_API_KEY for authentication.

title: Integrate Vertex AI Gemini into hello_world skill
description: Integrate Gemini into the hello_world skill using the new Go GenAI SDK ('google.golang.org/genai') with a Vertex AI backend. Support .env for config (GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT, GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION, GEMINI_MODEL) and replace the static greeting with a dynamic Gemini prompt.

title: Build a2a-grpc-echo multi-transport reference server
description:
Create a minimal echo A2A server that serves ALL THREE transports (gRPC, JSONRPC, REST/HTTP+JSON) from one binary, advertising all in the AgentCard SupportedInterfaces. Echo executor reflects message parts back as named artifacts (like a2acli serve --echo).

PURPOSE: a2acli has NO sister agent serving gRPC or REST — its gRPC/REST client paths are only tested against the a2a-go TCK SUT. This server becomes the real-agent test target for transport auto-selection (gRPC > JSONRPC > REST) and explicit --transport grpc/jsonrpc/rest.

Deterministic (no LLM) so a2acli e2e can assert exact output. Built on a2a-go v2 (match version with a2acli, currently v2.3.1).

Tracked for a2acli consumption as a2ac-k9i. See a2acli docs/TEST_AGENTS.md.

title: Build a2a-multimodal kitchen-sink reference server
description:
Create a deterministic agent returning EVERY artifact type (Text, Data/JSON, Raw bytes, FileURL) and able to drive EVERY task state (working, input-required, auth-required, completed, failed, canceled) on demand via skill ID or keyword.

PURPOSE: no current agent produces all artifact types or drives all task states. Complements the existing multimodal_echo skill (which only reflects input) by PRODUCING all types deterministically. Lets a2acli e2e assert: binary artifact save (a2ac-mfd) across all types, task-state handling, input-required/auth-required resumption.

Skills:
  all-artifacts : one of each part type in a single task
  state-<name>  : drive task to a specific state (working/input-required/auth-required/completed/failed/canceled)

Deterministic, no LLM. Built on a2a-go v2. Tracked for a2acli consumption as a2ac-ih8. See a2acli docs/TEST_AGENTS.md.

title: Restructure to multi-service layout (cmd/<service>/) for test fixtures
description:
a2a-simple is becoming the home for a2acli's deterministic A2A test fixtures (per a2acli docs/TEST_AGENTS.md). It's currently a single server/ binary. Restructure into a multi-service layout so multiple reference servers coexist:

  cmd/server/        (the existing 'Refactored A2A Agent' — move from server/)
  cmd/grpc-echo/     (new — multi-transport echo, see follow-up task)
  cmd/multimodal/    (new — kitchen-sink artifact/state producer)
  cmd/extended-card/ (new — extendedAgentCard target)

Keep shared helpers (auth interceptor, skill patterns) in an internal/ package reusable across services. Update README + Makefile with per-service build targets.

This unblocks the deterministic-fixture services that a2acli's e2e suite will consume.

title: Switch to net.Listen + http.Serve from http.ListenAndServe
notes:
Replace http.ListenAndServe in server/main.go with net.Listen + http.Serve pattern.

Benefits: actual bound address available before serving starts (useful for port 0 / test scenarios), cleaner error separation between bind and serve failures.

Reference: ~/projects/apex/tools/sample_servers/a2a_server/main.go (listener/Serve pattern). Best done alongside a2a-simple-tfe (logging) since both touch main().
Depends on a2a-simple-tfe (logging); both touch server/main.go, ship together.
close_reason: Implemented: net.Listen+http.Serve replacing http.ListenAndServe in server/main.go; bound address logged via listener.Addr().

title: Adopt slog structured logging and a2asrv.NewLoggingInterceptor
close_reason: Implemented: slog+a2alog.AttachFormatter+NewLoggingInterceptor in server/main.go; all log.Printf migrated to slog in main.go and skills.go; --level/--log-file/--payload flags added.

title: Add multimodal_echo skill for a2acli --parts/--json/--file testing
description:
Add a 'multimodal_echo' skill to a2a-simple that inspects all received message parts and echoes them back as artifacts, preserving part type, mediaType, and content.

PURPOSE: provides Option B test target for a2acli a2ac-79d (multi-modal message input -- --parts, --json, --file, --data flags). The skill returns each received part as a separate artifact so callers can assert round-trip fidelity.

SKILL BEHAVIOUR:
- TextPart: echo as text artifact with prefix 'text: '
- DataPart: return as JSON artifact, include mediaType in artifact name
- FilePart/RawPart: return bytes as artifact with original mediaType
- Return a summary artifact listing all part types received

AGENTCARD: add to skills list as:
  {ID: 'multimodal_echo', Name: 'Multimodal Echo',
   Description: 'Echoes all received message parts back as artifacts. Use to validate multi-modal message construction.'}

TEST (once a2acli a2ac-79d is implemented):
  a2acli send 'test' --file ./data.json --data '{"key":"val"}' --skill multimodal_echo --wait --output json
  # assert artifacts contain the file bytes and data JSON
close_reason: Implemented: handleMultimodalEcho in server/skills.go and server/main.go

title: Support TaskID continuation in CLI
description: Add a --task flag to the 'invoke' command. When provided, the client should set the taskId field in the outgoing Message to enable session continuity.

title: Implement AI Researcher skill with Task updates and Artifacts

title: Build a2a-extended-card reference server
description:
Create an agent advertising capabilities.extendedAgentCard: true that returns a richer AgentCard to authenticated callers via GetExtendedAgentCard. Public well-known card is minimal; extended card (gated behind auth) has more skills/detail.

PURPOSE: the concrete test target unblocking a2acli a2ac-o2i (discover --extended), currently held for lack of any server supporting extended cards.

Built on a2a-go v2. Tracked for a2acli consumption as a2ac-mym. See a2acli docs/TEST_AGENTS.md.

title: Review and Polish A2A Blog Post
description: Read through the drafted blog post, check for clarity, and prepare for publication.

title: Implement Gemini-based Intent Detector for Skill Dispatching
description: Replace the manual string matching in the server with a Gemini-powered router. It should use the AgentCard's skill descriptions to decide which SkillID to invoke based on user input.
notes:
Implementation notes (added 2026-06-29):

APPROACH: Add a Gemini-powered classify step before the string-match dispatch in Execute(). genaiClient is already on agentExecutor, so no new dependencies. Send a structured prompt listing all AgentCard skill IDs and descriptions, ask for a single skill ID back.

TRADE-OFF: Every request that doesn't arrive with an explicit skillId metadata key pays an extra Gemini round-trip before work starts. At 7 skills this is avoidable overhead; becomes genuinely useful at ~15+ skills or when descriptions overlap semantically.

OPEN QUESTIONS:
1. Should this be opt-in (BEADS_SMART_DISPATCH=1 env var or --smart-dispatch flag) so the latency cost is controllable per deployment?
2. What is the fallback when Gemini returns an unrecognised skill ID? Current string-match default to 'chat' — preserve that fallback or return an error?
3. Should the classifier use the same genaiClient/model as other skills, or a faster/cheaper dedicated model (e.g., flash-lite)?
4. Does the intent detector replace or layer over the skillId metadata shortcut? Recommendation: metadata shortcut always wins (zero latency for programmatic clients), classifier only fires when no metadata skillId is present.

title: Add read_aloud skill delegating to read-aloud A2A agent
description: Once read-aloud is refactored into a library, implement a new 'read_aloud' skill in the A2A server. This skill should accept multi-modal input, stream status updates via TaskStatusUpdateEvents, and return the final MP3 as an A2A Artifact.
notes:
Blocked on read-aloud library epic (read-aloud-89j). Key sub-tasks to watch:
- read-aloud-89j.2: dependency injection of GCP clients via Config (needed so a2a-simple can supply its own genai/TTS clients)
- read-aloud-89j.3: in-memory string + io.Reader inputs (skill will pass text, not a file path)
- read-aloud-89j.5: public Go module path (needed for the replace directive or proper import)

Secondary dependency: read-aloud-of3.1 (split CLI into cmd/read-aloud; keep root as library only) — once that lands the pkg/ packages are cleanly importable without pulling in the Bubble Tea UI.

Re-evaluate when 89j.2, 89j.3, and 89j.5 are closed.
REVISED DIRECTION (2026-06-29):

The original approach (import read-aloud as a library) is superseded. read-aloud will ship its own A2A server as an output of its of3 epic (agent-aware CLI). Once that exists, this skill should demonstrate agent-to-agent delegation rather than library import.

NEW APPROACH:
- read-aloud A2A agent runs as a separate process/service
- a2a-simple 'read_aloud' skill accepts text or file input from the client, forwards it to the read-aloud A2A agent via an A2A tasks/send call, streams TaskStatusUpdateEvents (working heartbeats) while the remote agent processes, and returns the final MP3 artifact passed through from the read-aloud agent

This demonstrates a more valuable A2A pattern: cross-agent task delegation and artifact passing — not just library wrapping.

BLOCKED ON: read-aloud A2A server existing (to be added to read-aloud project, separate from 89j library work).

Drop any dependency on 89j sub-tasks. Previous notes about 89j.2/89j.3/89j.5 are no longer relevant to this issue.
